title: callista gracilicaulis
side_effects: Callista gracilicaulis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing in some individuals. If ingested in large quantities, it can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Long-term exposure to the plant's toxins can also lead to kidney damage and liver failure in severe cases. Furthermore, improper use of Callista gracilicaulis can also cause cardiovascular problems, including irregular heartbeats and blood pressure fluctuations.
